description: V2 oracle test facts for conservation laws in Hamiltonian mechanics. Length-balanced distractors (ratio 0.8-1.2x) to eliminate length bias.

facts.context: For an autonomous Hamiltonian system with H(q, p), the quantity H is:
facts.truth: conserved along trajectories (dH/dt = 0 via Hamilton's equations)
distractors: monotonically decreasing toward the global energy minimum state
conserved only when the system has a single degree of freedom
approximately constant but drifts due to symplectic corrections

facts.context: Liouville's theorem states that for Hamiltonian systems:
facts.truth: phase space volume is preserved under time evolution
distractors: trajectories converge to the lowest-energy fixed point
the total energy monotonically decreases over time
phase space volume contracts at a rate set by entropy

facts.context: The mathematical structure that underlies Liouville's theorem is:
facts.truth: symplectic geometry - the 2-form omega = Sum dq_i wedge dp_i is preserved
distractors: Riemannian geometry - the metric tensor g_ij on configuration space is preserved
affine geometry - the connection Gamma^k_ij on the tangent bundle is preserved
contact geometry - the 1-form alpha = Sum p_i dq_i minus H dt is preserved

facts.context: The first Poincare integral invariant oint p dq is:
facts.truth: conserved for any loop in phase space evolving under Hamiltonian flow
distractors: conserved only for loops that enclose a single equilibrium point
equal to the total energy integrated over the period of the orbit
defined only for systems that are completely integrable by quadrature

facts.context: Noether's theorem relates conservation laws to:
facts.truth: continuous symmetries of the Lagrangian (time -> energy, space -> momentum, rotation -> angular momentum)
distractors: discrete parity symmetries of the Hamiltonian (reflection -> charge, inversion -> helicity, swap -> spin)
topological invariants of the phase portrait (winding number -> action, genus -> stability, index -> period)
boundary conditions on the equations of motion (Dirichlet -> energy, Neumann -> momentum, mixed -> angular momentum)

facts.context: For the Kepler problem (gravitational two-body), angular momentum L is conserved because of:
distractors: translational invariance of the gravitational field
time-reversal symmetry of the orbital equations
scale invariance of the inverse-square force law

facts.context: The Laplace-Runge-Lenz vector A = p x L - mu*m*r_hat is conserved for:
facts.truth: the Kepler problem only (unique to 1/r potential, reflects hidden SO(4) symmetry)
distractors: all central force problems (generic to any V(r) potential, reflects standard SO(3) symmetry)
harmonic oscillator systems (unique to r^2 potential, reflects hidden SU(3) symmetry)
all spherically symmetric systems (generic to radial potentials, reflects full O(4) symmetry)

facts.context: Conservation of the Laplace-Runge-Lenz vector implies:
facts.truth: closed elliptical orbits that don't precess (orbits return to same point)
distractors: all bounded orbits must be perfectly circular with constant orbital speed
orbital eccentricity increases monotonically until the orbit becomes hyperbolic
the orbital period is independent of energy and depends only on angular momentum

facts.context: For an integrable Hamiltonian system, action variables J_i = (1/2pi) oint p_i dq_i are:
facts.truth: adiabatic invariants that remain constant even under slow parameter changes
distractors: topological invariants that classify the genus of the orbit in phase space
thermodynamic potentials that minimize under Hamiltonian evolution naturally
differential invariants that measure the curvature of trajectories in state space

facts.context: The KAM theorem for nearly integrable Hamiltonian systems states:
facts.truth: most invariant tori survive small perturbations (chaos doesn't immediately destroy all structure)
distractors: all invariant tori are destroyed by arbitrarily small perturbations (chaos onset is immediate)
perturbations convert all quasi-periodic orbits into stable periodic attracting cycles
only resonant tori survive perturbation while all non-resonant tori become chaotic orbits

facts.context: The Henon-Heiles system H = (px^2 + py^2)/2 + (x^2 + y^2)/2 + x^2*y - y^3/3 is:
facts.truth: non-integrable and exhibits chaos at high energies (only energy is globally conserved)
distractors: completely integrable with a hidden second integral quadratic in the momenta
chaotic at all energies with no conserved quantities including the Hamiltonian
integrable at high energies where the cubic coupling terms become negligible

facts.context: For a chaotic Hamiltonian system, Liouville's theorem implies:
facts.truth: phase space volume is still conserved even though trajectories are chaotic
distractors: phase space volume contracts to a fractal strange attractor over long times
the conservation of volume breaks down once Lyapunov exponents become positive
volume is conserved on average but fluctuates randomly around the mean value

facts.context: A canonical transformation (q, p) -> (Q, P) preserves:
facts.truth: the symplectic 2-form and Poisson brackets ({Q_i, P_j} = delta_ij)
distractors: the Riemannian metric and geodesic distances (ds^2 = g_ij dx^i dx^j)
the total kinetic energy and individual momentum components separately
the Hamiltonian function exactly without any generating function correction

facts.context: Generating functions for canonical transformations are related to:
facts.truth: Hamilton-Jacobi theory and the principal function S(q, alpha, t)
distractors: Lagrange multiplier methods and the constraint function Lambda(q, p, t)
Routh reduction theory and the modified Lagrangian R(q, beta, t)
Noether current analysis and the symmetry generator G(q, gamma, t)

facts.context: A Hamiltonian system with n degrees of freedom is completely integrable if it has:
facts.truth: n independent integrals of motion in involution (Liouville-Arnold theorem)
distractors: 2n independent integrals that span the full phase space (Darboux theorem)
n^2 independent constants constraining all coordinate pairs (Frobenius theorem)
n periodic orbits forming a basis for the motion (Poincare-Birkhoff theorem)

facts.context: The Poisson bracket {F, H} of a quantity F with the Hamiltonian gives:
facts.truth: the time derivative dF/dt (F is conserved iff {F, H} = 0)
distractors: the spatial gradient grad(F) projected onto the energy surface
the commutator of the associated quantum mechanical operators
the Lie derivative of F along the configuration space geodesic
